If exit code is 1: proceed to the steps below.

---

## 🚨 CRITICAL: HOW TO UPDATE THE QUEUE

NEVER use the Edit tool on content-queue.json — the file is large (300KB+) and frequently modified by other agents. Exact-text matching will fail.

INSTEAD, use queue-update.py for ALL queue modifications:
```
python3 /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/queue-update.py <item_id> field=value field2=value2
```
Examples:
```
python3 /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/queue-update.py my-article-id status=approved scheduledFor=2026-03-03T10:00:00Z
python3 /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/queue-update.py my-article-id status=rejected rejectedReason=poor_title rejectionNote=Title is too vague rejectedAt=2026-03-02T18:00:00Z
```
This is the ONLY approved method for updating queue items. Run a separate command per item.

---

## STEP 0 — SELF-HEALING CLEANUP (run first, always)
Read /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/content-queue.json. Find all items where status='post_failed' AND tweetId is a non-empty string. For each: use queue-update.py to set status=published, add publishedAt=now if missing. Self-heals tweets that posted but weren't marked published. Items with post_failed but NO tweetId are genuine failures — leave them.

REVIEW + SCHEDULE TASK: Read /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/content-queue.json and do the following:

## STEP 1 — REVIEW DRAFTS
Find all items with status 'draft'.

### For ARTICLES (type != 'tweet'):

First, check required fields — reject immediately with rejectionReason if any are missing:
- title, slug, excerpt, htmlContent, heroImage, heroImagePrompt

If heroImage field is present, also verify the ima

agent:atlas:cron:6cef6a39-3fde-4575-9208.task:
[cron:6cef6a39-3fde-4575-9208-947d53f6a425 atlas-debug] Debug task: On the agents page (https://cliffcircuit.ai/portal/agents.html), when you hover over any job row in the expanded Scheduled Jobs table, the entire row blinks/flickers. This does NOT happen on the old agents page (portal-old.html). The repo is at /Users/openclaw/workspace/cliffcircuit-ai/. The file is portal/agents.html. Investigate the CSS causing the flicker on hover (likely related to transition or background changes during re-render), fix it, commit and push to main. Compare with how portal-old.html handles hover if needed.
Current time: Friday, March 6th, 2026 — 1:27 PM (America/Los_Angeles)

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.
agent:atlas:cron:6cef6a39-3fde-4575-9208.response:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Root cause:** `renderAgentsPage()` and `renderCondensedData()` were unconditionally doing `tbody.innerHTML = ...` on every data refresh (realtime events, `enrichCronTokens` callback, polling). This destroyed and recreated all `<tr>` elements each cycle. When hovering over a row, the element under the cursor gets destroyed mid-hover → `:hover` state resets → new element appears without hover → browser re-applies hover = visible flicker/blink.

Portal-old.html doesn't have this issue because its cron table rendering is structured differently and doesn't do full innerHTML replacement on every poll cycle.

**Fix:** Both the full view and condensed view now build the new HTML string first and compare it against `tbody.innerHTML` before assigning. If the content hasn't changed (which is the common case between refreshes), the DOM is left untouched — preserving hover state and eliminating the flicker.

**Changes:** `portal/agents.html` — 2 locations (full view job table, condensed view job table). Committed and pushed to main (`cdb7560`).

agent:atlas:cron:befa343c-3f24-40bf-97ab.task:
[cron:befa343c-3f24-40bf-97ab-102954820866 atlas-refactor] Fix the agent portrait cards in portal/tokens.html (in the "Agent Sessions - Summary" section). Two issues:

## 1. Merge duplicate Cliff entries
Sessions with agent_id "main" and agent_id "cliff" are the same agent (Cliff). Merge them into a single card. When building the portrait data, treat "main" and "cliff" as the same agent. Use "cliff" as the display name and the cliff avatar.

## 2. Fix the card layout — it looks terrible
Current state: cards overflow horizontally, costs get cut off, no proper wrapping. Fix:
- Use CSS grid or flexbox that wraps properly at any width
- Cards should be a consistent size, not stretch or overflow
- Each card needs: avatar image (small, like 48x48), agent name, sessions count, tokens count, cost — all visible, nothing cut off
- On wider screens: cards in a single row if they fit, otherwise wrap to next row
- On mobile: stack vertically or 2-per-row
- Keep the dark theme styling consistent with the rest of the page
- Avatar images are at paths like /portal/cliff-avatar.jpg, /portal/samantha-avatar.jpg, /portal/scout-avatar.jpg, /portal/atlas-avatar.jpg

The known agent_id to display name mapping:
- "main" or "cliff" → "Cliff" (avatar: cliff-avatar.jpg)
- "samantha" → "Samantha" (avatar: samantha-avatar.jpg)  
- "scout" → "Scout" (avatar: scout-avatar.jpg)
- "atlas" → "Atlas" (avatar: atlas-avatar.jpg)

Any other agent_id that appears should still get a card, just without a custom avatar.

After changes, commit with message "fix: merge duplicate cliff, fix portrait card layout" and push to origin main.

agent:atlas:cron:da0de825-baa9-4356-989c.task:
[cron:da0de825-baa9-4356-989c-24aa5a2b370b atlas-refactor] Fix portal/portal-common.js to build the CRON_UUID_MAP dynamically instead of hardcoding it.

## Problem
CRON_UUID_MAP is a hardcoded object mapping cron UUIDs to names. Every time a cron is recreated with a new UUID, the map breaks and token data shows as 0 on the agents page. This keeps happening.

## Fix
1. Keep the hardcoded CRON_UUID_MAP as a fallback for historical sessions
2. At page load, fetch the live cron list from `https://api.cliffcircuit.ai/sessions.json` (the response has a `crons` array where each entry has `id` and `name`)
3. Merge the live cron data INTO CRON_UUID_MAP so new UUIDs are automatically resolved
4. Make CRON_UUID_MAP a `let` instead of `const` if needed, or use Object.assign to merge

The fetch is simple:
```js
fetch("https://api.cliffcircuit.ai/sessions.json?t=" + Date.now())
  .then(r => r.json())
  .then(d => {
    (d.crons || []).forEach(c => {
      if (c.id && c.name) CRON_UUID_MAP[c.id] = c.name;
    });
  });
```

This should run early in page init so the map is populated before enrichCronTokens runs.

## Files to edit
- portal/portal-common.js — add the dynamic fetch, change const to let if needed

## Do NOT
- Remove the existing hardcoded entries (they cover historical sessions)
- Change any other files

Commit: "fix: dynamically populate CRON_UUID_MAP from live cron list" and push to origin main.
Current time: Friday, March 6th, 2026 — 9:36 PM (America/Los_Angeles)

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

agent:atlas:cron:47cfaf3a-ca98-4eb5-9851.response:
Done. Here's what changed in `portal/agents.html`:

**Token map logic** — Instead of blindly keeping the first (most recent) session per cron name, it now checks: if the existing entry has < 1,000 tokens and the current row has more, replace it. This means recreated crons with empty placeholder sessions will get swapped out for the older session that has real token data (e.g. 422K tokens). Crons that already have meaningful data in their most recent session are unaffected.

**Query limits** — Both `session_snapshots` queries bumped from `.limit(200)` to `.limit(500)` to catch historical sessions from old UUIDs.

**Removed `.gt('total_tokens', 0)` filter** — So we still see placeholder sessions (needed to iterate through them), but the new logic ensures they get replaced by sessions with real data.

Pushed as `156f511` to `origin main`.

## 1. Add info tip tooltips to the raw table (Agent Sessions — Raw)
The summary table has info tips (ℹ) next to task names but the raw table does not. Add the same info tip tooltip next to each task name in the raw table rows. Use the same _getTaskTooltip function and .info-tip CSS class already in the file. The raw table task cell is around line 739 — add the tooltip span after the task name text.

## 2. Fix "Atlas Cron" display name
The function sessionKeyToFriendly in portal-common.js falls back to "Atlas Cron" when it cannot resolve a cron UUID. This happens because the dynamic CRON_UUID_MAP fetch has not completed when the page first renders.

Fix in portal-common.js: in the agentCronMatch block (around line 302-308), instead of falling back to agentLabel + " Cron", extract the cron UUID from the key and try to look it up. If still not found, use a more descriptive fallback like the agent name + the task type from the cron name. The key format is: agent:<agentId>:cron:<uuid>:run:<runId>

Better yet: when the dynamic fetch completes and updates CRON_UUID_MAP, trigger a re-render so that any "Atlas Cron" labels get replaced with proper names. Add a callback or event after the fetch in portal-common.js that pages can hook into.

The simplest approach: make the dynamic CRON_UUID_MAP fetch in portal-common.js use await (wrap in an async IIFE or make it a function that portal pages call before rendering). That way the map is populated BEFORE any rendering happens.

## Files to edit
- portal/tokens.html (add info tips to raw table)
- portal/portal-common.js (fix the timing of dynamic UUID fetch)

2. **Await before render:** Added `await Promise.all([_sessionLabelsReady, _cronMapReady])` in `initPortal()` so both session labels and cron UUID mappings are fully loaded before any page rendering begins. This eliminates the "Atlas Cron" fallback appearing because the map hadn't loaded yet.

agent:atlas:cron:ce615ff6-3724-4fb9-bc9c.task:
[cron:ce615ff6-3724-4fb9-bc9c-51ccd3d619d8 atlas-debug] Fix the recycle pipeline precheck scripts so they handle ALL rejected items in Supabase.

## CONTEXT
Scripts are at /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/
Supabase has 14 rejected articles. Current scripts only find 2-3 of them due to missing reasons.

## PROBLEM 1 — recycle-media-precheck.js
File: /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/recycle-media-precheck.js
Line 15: MEDIA_REASONS = ['missing_hero_image', 'bad_hero_image']
Missing: 'hero_image_missing_on_disk', 'hero_image_not_found'
Fix: Add those two to MEDIA_REASONS.

Also, the script skips 'orphaned' items not found in content-queue.json. This is too strict — Supabase is the source of truth. Remove the orphan skip entirely (keep the markAttempted loop-prevention, remove the queueIds.has check that causes early skip).

## PROBLEM 2 — recycle-precheck.js
File: /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/recycle-precheck.js
Line ~28: FIXABLE_REASONS = ['poor_title', 'title_too_long', 'poor_excerpt', 'missing_htmlContent', 'content_empty_or_too_short', 'tweet_hook_missing', 'wrong_template']
Missing: 'title_trailing_period', 'published_without_content'
Fix: Add both to FIXABLE_REASONS.

ALSO: The rejected_reason field in Supabase sometimes has COMMA-SEPARATED multiple reasons like 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59), hero_image_not_found'. The precheck needs to split on ', ' and check if ANY token starts with a FIXABLE_REASON prefix (not exact match, because the reason may have details like 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59)').

Current code does: reasons.filter(r => FIXABLE_REASONS.includes(r)) — this fails on 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59)' because exact match misses.
Fix: Change the filter to: reasons.filter(r => FIXABLE_REASONS.some(fr => r === fr || r.startsWith(fr + ' ') || r.startsWith(fr + ',')))

Apply same prefix-matching fix to recycle-media-precheck.js MEDIA_REASONS check.

## AFTER FIXING
Run node recycle-precheck.js and node r
[truncated]

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Done. Here's what changed in `portal/tokens.html`:

**Change 1 — Stack order: highest cost at bottom**
- Previously, segments in each bar used a global sort order (`sortedSegKeys`) based on total cost across all time periods
- Now each bar independently sorts its segments by that bar's cost values (`sort((a, b) => b.cost - a.cost)`) — largest at bottom, smallest at top
- The visual rendering reverses the sorted array since CSS `flex-direction: column` places the first child at top

**Change 2 — Interactive legend toggles**
- Added `_costChartHidden` state object (`{ agent: Set(), model: Set() }`) to track hidden segments per view mode
- Legend items now have `cursor: pointer`, `data-seg` attributes, and click delegation via `legendEl.onclick`
- Clicking a legend item calls `_toggleCostLegend()` which adds/removes the segment key from the hidden set and re-renders
- Hidden items get the `.cost-legend-hidden` class: `opacity: 0.35` + strikethrough on the label
- When segments are hidden, the grid and bar heights rescale to the visible max cost so bars fill the chart properly
- Tooltips only show visible segments; totals reflect only what's shown
- State persists independently for "By Agent" and "By Model" views

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Done. Here's the summary:

**What changed (commit 7a3848b):**

Individual session rows in the Agent Sessions table are now clickable. Clicking a row expands an inline detail panel below it showing:

1. **Timestamps** — Sent at (first_seen_at) and Completed at (last_seen_at), formatted with day/date/time
2. **Duration** and session **Kind** (direct, cron, etc.)
3. **Model** — full friendly name with thinking level if set
4. **Token Breakdown** — input, output, cache read, cache write, context window usage (% used)
5. **Cost Breakdown** — per-category costs (input, output, cache read, cache write) + total
6. **Task / Response** — fetched via gateway WebSocket `sessions.preview` method, with static JSON fallback
7. **Session ID** — subtle monospace text at the bottom (session key + UUID)

**Technical details:**
- Extended the Supabase query to fetch 21 columns (was 13) — adds kind, thinking_level, context_tokens, percent_used, and per-category cost fields
- Processed items now carry `_raw` reference and `first_seen_at`/`last_seen_at` fields
- Panel is collapsible — clicking the same row again closes it, clicking another opens a new one
- CSS was already in place from a prior skeleton; JS logic fully implemented
- Gateway WebSocket connection is attempted for task/response content; gracefully falls back if auth is required or gateway is unreachable

**What changed (commit 7a3848b):**

Individual session rows in the Agent Sessions table are now clickable. Clicking a row expands an inline detail panel below it showing:

1. **Timestamps** — Sent at (first_seen_at) and Completed at (last_seen_at), formatted with day/date/time
2. **Duration** and session **Kind** (direct, cron, etc.)
3. **Model** — full friendly name with thinking level if set
4. **Token Breakdown** — input, output, cache read, cache write, context window usage (% used)
5. **Cost Breakdown** — per-category costs (input, output, cache read, cache write) + total
6. **Task / Response** — fetched via gateway WebSocket `sessions.preview` method, with static JSON fallback
7. **Session ID** — subtle monospace text at the bottom (session key + UUID)

**Technical details:**
- Extended the Supabase query to fetch 21 columns (was 13) — adds kind, thinking_level, context_tokens, percent_used, and per-category cost fields
- Processed items now carry `_raw` reference and `first_seen_at`/`last_seen_at` fields
- Panel is collapsible — clicking the same row again closes it, clicking another opens a new one

**What changed:**

1. **Created `session-previews.js`** (`~/.openclaw/workspace/bin/`) — reads session JSONL files across all agents, extracts first user message (task) and last assistant message (response), writes `portal/session-previews.json` keyed by session_key and session_id. Limits to 7-day window, truncates at 2000 chars.

2. **Updated `_fetchSessionPreview` in `tokens.html`** — now tries the static `session-previews.json` first (faster, no auth needed) with a 60-second in-memory cache to avoid re-fetching on each panel open. Gateway WebSocket is the fallback. Improved fallback messaging when neither source has data.

3. **Created cron job** (`session-previews-refresh`) — runs every 10 minutes via Haiku to regenerate the static preview file and push to GitHub.

4. **Deployed** — commit `00de0cd` pushed to main + session-previews.json initial data already deployed.

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
All three fixes deployed (commit `1c11e0e`):

1. **Session detail from Supabase** — `_fetchSessionPreview` now queries `atlas_jobs` for `task_prompt`/`response_summary` as the primary source, falling back to static JSON then gateway WS. Sessions before March 7 get a clear "not available" message.

2. **Right bar clipping fixed** — Added `overflow: visible` to `.cost-chart-wrap`, `.cost-chart-container`, and `.cost-chart`, plus increased right padding from 12px to 24px.

3. **Time range label** — Added a `cost-chart-range-label` element below the chart title that dynamically shows the active filter (e.g. "Today", "Last 7 Days"). Updates on every render via the `_rangeLabels` map in `cost-chart.js`.

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Two changes to portal/tokens.html session detail interaction:**

1. **Summary table — single-session tasks now clickable:** The `_renderRunsCell` function now detects count=1 and renders the count as a clickable span (dotted underline, cursor:pointer) that opens the full session detail panel directly via `_toggleSingleSessionDetail`. Uses a stable sid cache so toggling off works correctly across re-renders.

2. **Raw table — all rows now clickable:** Every row in `renderRawTable` now has `cursor:pointer` and an `onclick` that calls `_toggleRawSessionDetail`, which delegates to the existing `_toggleSessionDetail`. The session data is registered in `_sessionDataMap` during render so the detail panel has access to all token/cost/model data.

Both use the same detail panel component, same styling, same 3-tier data fetching (atlas_jobs → session-previews.json → gateway WS). Committed and pushed — auto-deploying to Cloudflare Pages.

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Done. Here's what changed:

**Summary table rows are now fully clickable** (commit `88ccf3f`, pushed to main):

1. **Clickable rows** — Every summary `<tr>` has `onclick` → `_handleSummaryRowClick()` which routes single-session rows to the detail panel and multi-session rows to expand/collapse. Buttons are excluded via `event.target.closest('button')` so the `+` button still works independently.

2. **Alignment fixed** — Removed the `<span>` wrappers with `display:inline-flex` and dotted-underline styling from session counts. Counts are now plain text inside the right-aligned `<td>`, so they align naturally with the column header.

3. **Visual feedback** — Added `cursor:pointer` to all summary rows. The existing `hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors` classes already provide the subtle hover background change (same as raw table rows).

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
**Done.** Fixed the cost bar chart time bucketing in `tokens.html` (commit `2a614d8`).

**What changed:** The `_renderStackedCostChart` function now spreads each session's cost, tokens, and counts proportionally across all time buckets (hours or days) the session overlaps, using an overlap-fraction calculation (`overlapMs / totalDuration`). Previously, the entire cost was placed in the bucket matching the session's start time — so a 12-hour session running 11 PM to 11 AM would pile all cost into the 11 PM bar. Now each hour gets its fair share.

**Key details:**
- Two new helpers: `_bucketStartMs` (finds bucket boundary) and `_bucketKeyAndLabel` (generates sort key + display label)
- Fast path preserved: sessions fitting within a single bucket skip the spreading loop
- Session count incremented once (in the first overlapping bucket), not fractionally
- Applies to all three views (By Agent, By Model, By Task) since they share the same bucketing logic
- Committed locally, not pushed — ready for review

**Done.** Fixed the cost chart out-of-range bucket issue in `portal/tokens.html` (commit `857b745`).

**What changed:** After the proportional cost spreading loop builds all buckets, a new filtering step computes the selected time range boundaries (using the same range logic as the main query) and deletes any bucket whose key falls outside `[rangeStart, rangeEnd]`. This means 'Last 1 Hour' now only shows the 1–2 buckets within that hour window, not the 12+ hours of spread-out cost from long-running sessions.

The fix is ~25 lines — no new dependencies, no structural changes. Committed locally, not pushed.

Done. Two fixes shipped (commit `00d6637`, deployed):

**FIX 1 — 10-minute granularity:** "Last 1 Hour" now renders 6 bars (one per 10-minute window) instead of a single hourly bar. `_getTimeBuckets` returns `'10min'` for the `1h` range, and both `_bucketStartMs` and `_bucketKeyAndLabel` floor minutes to the nearest 10 with labels like `12:00`, `12:10`, etc.

The sum of all bars matches the header Total Cost — no more "missing money" from sessions spanning beyond the visible window. Simplified the bucketing loop from ~25 lines to 6.

agent:atlas:cron:ce615ff6-3724-4fb9-bc9c.response:
**Done.** Here's the summary:

**What changed:** Restored proportional cost spreading in `portal/tokens.html` `_renderStackedCostChart`. The previous commit (00d6637) had replaced the proportional algorithm with a simpler "place all cost in end bucket" approach, which caused long sessions to show as one giant spike instead of distributing across their active time.

**The fix (commit eacb063):**
- Sessions shorter than one bucket → fast path, cost goes in one bucket (unchanged)
- Sessions spanning multiple buckets → cursor walks from `_bucketStartMs(startTs)` through `endMs`, calculating overlap fraction per bucket and allocating `fraction * totalCost` to each
- Session count incremented only in the first overlapping bucket
- Tokens (in/out) also spread proportionally
- 10-minute bucket support (`bucketMode = '10min'`, `bucketSizeMs = 600000`) fully preserved
- Post-spreading range filtering still removes out-of-range buckets

**Note:** Because cost is now spread proportionally and then range-filtered, bar totals may be slightly less than the header Total Cost when a session extends outside the visible range. This is the expected trade-off vs. the misleading spike behavior.

Done. Here's what changed:

**Cost bar chart now uses `session_turns` instead of `session_snapshots`.**

- Added `_fetchSessionTurns()` — queries the `session_turns` table for the selected time range with all active filters (agent, provider, model, task)
- Made `renderCostBarChart()` async — it fetches turn data independently, then passes it to the chart renderer

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
All three fixes deployed:

1. **Title attributes removed** — stripped `title="${esc(g.task)}"` from summary table and `title="${esc(s.label || '')}"` from raw table task cells. No more browser-native popups fighting the custom ⓘ tooltips.

2. **Raw table tooltips working** — the info-tip spans were already in the render code, but the `title` attribute was creating a competing native tooltip that obscured them. With the `title` removed, the CSS `::after` tooltips now display cleanly.

3. **Dynamic cron schedules** — removed all hardcoded schedule/frequency text from every `_taskTooltips` entry (affected: Scheduler, Portal Refresh, Pipeline Health, Weekly Report, Live Sessions Refresh, Session Previews Refresh, and others with time references). Added `_getCronSchedule()` which reverse-maps display names through `CRON_DISPLAY` back to cron names, then looks up the `schedule` field from `window._cronList`. Added `_fmtCronSchedule()` to format cron expressions and `every Xm` patterns into readable strings. `_getTaskTooltip()` now appends ` · Schedule: every 5m` (etc.) at the end when a cron schedule is found. Non-cron sessions (DMs, subagents) show no schedule line.

Here's the summary:

**🏗️ OpenClaw Dashboard — Setup Complete**

**URL:** `https://dashboard.cliffcircuit.com`

**What was done:**
1. Cloned `tugcantopaloglu/openclaw-dashboard` to `/Users/openclaw/workspace/openclaw-dashboard`
2. No npm install needed — pure Node.js, zero dependencies
3. Created launchd plist (`com.openclaw.dashboard`) running on port 3200 with KeepAlive
4. Added ingress rule to the existing `openclaw` Cloudflare Tunnel (no new tunnel needed)
5. Created DNS CNAME `dashboard.cliffcircuit.com` → tunnel UUID

**Adjustments from the original plan:**
- **Domain:** Used `dashboard.cliffcircuit.com` instead of `cliffcircuit.com/dashboard` — the app doesn't support base-path routing, so a subdomain is required
- **Zone:** Used `.com` zone instead of `.ai` — none of the stored API tokens (cloudflare-cache-purge, cloudflare-api-token, cloudflare-pages-token) have DNS write permissions for the `.ai` zone. The tunnel cert only covers `.com`
- **Tunnel:** Reused the existing `openclaw` tunnel rather than creating a separate one

**Recovery token:** `aada7ff7e27248d3bb22535238f401b5` (saved in dashboard.log)

**Next steps for Tim:**
1. Visit `https://dashboard.cliffcircuit.com` and complete first-time registration (username + password)
2. After logging in, enable TOTP MFA in settings (Google Authenticator compatible)
3. Consider creating a Cloudflare API token with DNS:Edit scope for the `cliffcircuit.ai` zone if you'd prefer `dashboard.cliffcircuit.ai`

# jMunch Integration

## Overview
Replaced full-file reads in Samantha cron prompts and Atlas task messages with jDocMunch/jCodeMunch queries for surgical context retrieval (~150-200 tokens vs 6K-28K tokens per file).

## Files Modified
- **Cron: samantha-media** (`4e1e60f5`) — replaced "Read AGENTS.md" with jDocMunch query for media phase instructions
- **Cron: samantha-writer** (`676edfac`) — replaced "Run WRITER phase" with explicit jDocMunch queries for writer phase + brand voice
- **Cron: samantha-recycle-writer** (`43a6704d`) — STEP 2 changed from reading RECYCLE_INSTRUCTIONS.md + CLIFF_VOICE.md to jDocMunch queries; all other steps preserved
- **Cron: samantha-recycle-media** (`50708c6f`) — STEP 2 changed from reading RECYCLE_INSTRUCTIONS.md to jDocMunch query; all other steps preserved
- **`/Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/bin/atlas-run.sh`** (~line 106) — added JCODEMUNCH_PREAMBLE variable prepended to every Atlas task message

## Key Details
- jDocMunch runs on port 3211, jCodeMunch on port 3210 (global MCP plugins)
- Tool names: `jdocmunch_jdocmunch_query` and `jcodemunch_jcodemunch_query`
- All agents already have these tools available via MCP
- Recycle cron steps 1, 3-5 were preserved exactly — only step 2 (file reads) changed

## Decisions
- Kept jCodeMunch preamble as a shell variable rather than hardcoding inline — easier to update later
- Used natural language queries in prompts (e.g., "samantha media phase hero image generation instructions") rather than exact section names, since jDocMunch handles semantic search
- Did NOT change model or thinking settings on any cron — only message content

## Open Items
[truncated]

# Project: Portal Token Usage — Cost Chart + Session Details
**Files:**
- `/Users/openclaw/workspace/cliffcircuit-ai/portal/tokens.html` — main page with inline chart JS
- `/Users/openclaw/workspace/cliffcircuit-ai/portal/portal-common.js` — shared Supabase client, constants, helpers
**Repo:** `CliffCircuit/cliffcircuit-ai` — deploy via `git add portal/tokens.html && git commit -m "..." && git push origin main`

## Current State
Cost bar chart uses `session_turns` Supabase table for granular per-turn cost data. Summary tables, raw table, and session detail panels use `session_snapshots`. For "Last 1 Hour" range, 10-minute buckets (6 bars); other short ranges hourly; longer ranges daily. Session detail panels fetch task/response/duration from Supabase `atlas_jobs` first, then `session-previews.json`, then gateway WebSocket. Sessions before March 7 2026 show "not available". Ghost sessions with 0/0 tokens filtered at query level. Token breakdown: Input (cache_read + input_tokens), Output, Cache Write (conditional), Total. Summary table SESSIONS column always renders a [+] button for consistent alignment. Tooltips use CSS `::after` with `data-tip` attribute — no browser-native `title` attributes on task cells. Schedule info in tooltips is dynamically appended from `window._cronList` via `_getCronSchedule()` and `_fmtCronSchedule()`.

## Key Functions — tokens.html (approximate line numbers)
- `_taskTooltips` (~L766) — static tooltip descriptions (no schedule info)
- `_getCronSchedule(displayName)` (~L826) — reverse-maps display name to cron entry, returns schedule string
- `_fmtCronSchedule(schedule)` (~L850) — formats cron expre
[truncated]

agent:atlas:cron:ce615ff6-3724-4fb9-bc9c.task:
[cron:ce615ff6-3724-4fb9-bc9c-51ccd3d619d8 atlas-debug] Fix the recycle pipeline precheck scripts so they handle ALL rejected items in Supabase.

## CONTEXT
Scripts are at /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/
Supabase has 14 rejected articles. Current scripts only find 2-3 of them due to missing reasons.

## PROBLEM 1 — recycle-media-precheck.js
File: /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/recycle-media-precheck.js
Line 15: MEDIA_REASONS = ['missing_hero_image', 'bad_hero_image']
Missing: 'hero_image_missing_on_disk', 'hero_image_not_found'
Fix: Add those two to MEDIA_REASONS.

Also, the script skips 'orphaned' items not found in content-queue.json. This is too strict — Supabase is the source of truth. Remove the orphan skip entirely (keep the markAttempted loop-prevention, remove the queueIds.has check that causes early skip).

## PROBLEM 2 — recycle-precheck.js
File: /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/samantha/recycle-precheck.js
Line ~28: FIXABLE_REASONS = ['poor_title', 'title_too_long', 'poor_excerpt', 'missing_htmlContent', 'content_empty_or_too_short', 'tweet_hook_missing', 'wrong_template']
Missing: 'title_trailing_period', 'published_without_content'
Fix: Add both to FIXABLE_REASONS.

ALSO: The rejected_reason field in Supabase sometimes has COMMA-SEPARATED multiple reasons like 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59), hero_image_not_found'. The precheck needs to split on ', ' and check if ANY token starts with a FIXABLE_REASON prefix (not exact match, because the reason may have details like 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59)').

Current code does: reasons.filter(r => FIXABLE_REASONS.includes(r)) — this fails on 'title_too_long (60 chars, max 59)' because exact match misses.
Fix: Change the filter to: reasons.filter(r => FIXABLE_REASONS.some(fr => r === fr || r.startsWith(fr + ' ') || r.startsWith(fr + ',')))

Apply same prefix-matching fix to recycle-media-precheck.js MEDIA_REASONS check.

## AFTER FIXING
Run node recycle-precheck.js and node r
[truncated]

   - Cleaned up unused `QUEUE_PATH` constant and queue-loading code

[Sun 2026-03-01 03:20 PST] [System Message] [sessionId: e4809786-986f-4688-aa6f-9c9b79ed468b] A cron job "samantha-cliff-tweets" just completed successfully.

Here's the run summary:

---

**📝 PHASE 4 COMPLETE — 6 CLIFF STANDALONE TWEETS DRAFTED**

All saved to `content-queue.json` as `draft`. Nothing posts until you approve.

**Slots used:** 09:00, 11:30, 14:00, 16:30, 19:00, 21:00 PT

---

**1 — 09:00** *(building in public / failure moment)*
Built an auto repair workflow with a time-based trigger: no contact by noon, fire an update text.

Advisor had already called at 9:30. The text went out anyway. Customer was confused.

Fixed it: trigger on repair order status changes, not the clock. Obvious in retrospect.

---

**2 — 11:30** *(pattern observation)*
Every service business article I write has the same villain.

Not the competition. Not pricing. Not Google reviews.

It's the silence between jobs. The follow-up that never happened. The client who went quiet and nobody noticed until they were already gone.

---

**3 — 14:00** *(counterintuitive simplicity)*
Two questions. That's the entire intelligence layer for emergency triage.

Is this an emergency or can we schedule a visit?
Is water actively running right now?

Everything after that is logistics. The hardest part of the AI is knowing when not to automate the answer.

---

**4 — 16:30** *(real lesson from restaurant research)*
Learned something building restaurant win-back workflows: putting a promotional offer in every message trains guests to wait for the deal.

First message: relationship. No offer.
Second message: offer if they haven't responded.

That distinction shows up in the margin.

---

**5 — 19:00** *(reliability vs. demos)*
The bar for an AI workflow isn't 'can it do this.'

It's 'will it do this at 9pm on a Friday when I'm not watching.'

A lot of demos clear the first bar. Very few clear the second one consistently.

---

**6 — 21:00** *(human buy-in)*
The part of building AI workflows 
[truncated]
agent:samantha:telegram:direct:main.response:
[[reply_to_current]] **7 more @CliffCircuit tweets** drafted and queued — all awaiting your approval.

**Tonight, Mar 1 @ 22:30** — Painting contractors think they lose bids on price. After tracing 30 actual estimates: almost never a cheaper bid. The job went to whoever called back first.

**Mar 2 @ 11:30** — Gym members don't decide to quit on cancellation day. The decision is made 3–4 weeks earlier — you can see it in check-in frequency, class bookings, unused guest passes. The signal is there before it hits the revenue report.

**Mar 2 @ 14:00** — Every workflow designed first is too complicated. The version that actually ships is always simpler: one trigger, one message. Does it work? Good. Now add the next layer.

**Mar 2 @ 16:30** — Vet clinic follow-up: "your pet is overdue for vaccinations" is accurate and counterproductive. The client already knows. It creates avoidance. Tone matters as much as timing.

**Mar 2 @ 19:00** — House cleaning clients are the politest churners in any service business. No complaint, no cancellation call — they just stop rebooking. By the time there's an empty slot, they've been with someone else for six weeks.

**Mar 2 @ 22:30** — Moving companies treat the booking confirmation as the finish line. It's actually the start of a 4–6 week window where the customer is still anxious, still seeing competitor ads. Most companies send nothing during that window.

**Mar 3 @ 11:30** — The sophisticated workflow isn't the one that gets used. The one that holds up is designed from the edge cases outward: what does it do when it fires at the wrong time, to the wrong person, in the wrong context?

No duplicates with existing queue. Approve in `content-queue.json` when ready.
